title: thymus × celtibericus nothosubsp. celtibericus
intro: Thymus × celtibericus nothosubsp. celtibericus is a nothosubspecies of thyme, a hybrid plant that is a cross between Thymus mastichina and Thymus pulegioides. It is known for its small, pink to purple flowers and its fragrant leaves, which have been used in traditional medicine for their antiseptic and antibacterial properties. The medicinal properties of Thymus × celtibericus nothosubsp. celtibericus include its ability to combat infections, reduce inflammation, and aid in digestion, making it a valuable herb for promoting overall health and well-being. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and essential oils, which can be used to support respiratory health, reduce stress and anxiety, and boost the immune system.
